title: Creamy Vietnamese Tofu Bowl
description: A fresh, creamy brunch‑style bowl that blends velvety tofu, a hint of cocoa, and bright Vietnamese accents for a harmonious, halal‑friendly meal.
instructions: Press the tofu to remove excess moisture, then cut into 1‑inch cubes.
In a bowl whisk together tamari, lime juice, brown sugar, and cocoa powder; add tofu and let marinate for 15 minutes to cure the flavors.
Heat a non‑stick skillet over medium heat. Add tofu cubes and cook, turning occasionally, until all sides are golden and slightly crisp, about 8 minutes.
In the same skillet, pour coconut milk, reduce heat to low, and simmer for 3 minutes until the sauce thickens slightly.
Transfer tofu and sauce to a serving bowl; top with fresh cilantro, green onions, chopped mint, and roasted peanuts.
Squeeze a little extra lime over the bowl and give a final gentle stir before serving.
